name: HNMS Jan van Brakel (M 80)
events: 15 Nov 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HrMs Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. Baron T.K. van Asbeck, RNN) and HMCS Rosthern (Lt. W. Russell, RCNR). (1)

16 Nov 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HrMs Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. Baron T.K. van Asbeck, RNN), HMCS Rosthern (Lt. W. Russell, RCNR), HMS Hildasay (T/Lt. W.P. Hart, RNR) and HMS Cape Argona (T/Lt. R.G. Wallace, RNR). (1)

17 Nov 1941 HrMs O 10 (Lt. Baron D.T. Mackay, RNN) participated in A/S exercises off Tobermory together with HrMs Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. Baron T.K. van Asbeck, RNN) and HMCS Rosthern (Lt. W. Russell, RCNR). (1)
9 Jan 1942 HrMs Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. Baron T.K. van Asbeck, RNN) is docked in the floating dock (AFD 3) at the Rosyth Dockyard. (2)
13 Jan 1942 HrMs Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. Baron T.K. van Asbeck, RNN) is undocked. (2)
21 Feb 1942 HMS Winchester (Lt.Cdr. D.J.B. Jewitt, RN) and HrMs Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. Baron T.K. van Asbeck, RNN) are docked in No.1 graving dock at the Rosyth Dockyard. (2)
12 Mar 1942 HMS Winchester (Lt.Cdr. D.J.B. Jewitt, RN) and HrMs Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. Baron T.K. van Asbeck, RNN) are undocked. (2)

9 Jun 1942 HNMS Jan van Brakel (Lt.Cdr. T.K. van Asbeck, RNN) picks up 34 survivors from the American tanker Franklin K. Lane that was torpedoed and sunk by German U-boat U-502 about 40 nautical miles north-north-east of La Guiara, Venezuela in position 11°12'N, 66°39'W.
